name: runpane
description: Thin installer and local control CLI for Pane
packageInstallPolicy: The packages must not download, install, or configure Pane during package installation.
Work starts only when a user runs `runpane ...`.

codex.title: Codex
codex.description: Open a Codex terminal tab and allow the initial input to drive the agent.

claude.title: Claude Code
claude.description: Open a Claude Code terminal tab and allow the initial input to drive the agent.

commands.name: help
commands.summary: Show help for runpane or a specific command.

commands.name: setup
commands.summary: Open the guided setup wizard for install, remote host setup, update, and diagnostics.

commands.name: install
commands.summary: Install Pane on this machine or configure this machine as a remote daemon host.

commands.name: update
commands.summary: Update the Pane desktop app using the same artifact path as install client.

commands.name: version
commands.summary: Print the runpane wrapper version without contacting, launching, or focusing Pane.

commands.name: doctor
commands.summary: Run platform, release, installed Pane, daemon reachability, and remote setup diagnostics.

commands.name: agents doctor
commands.summary: Diagnose whether a built-in agent command is available in a Pane repository environment.

commands.name: agent-context
commands.summary: Print token-efficient Pane command context for coding agents.

commands.name: repos list
commands.summary: List repositories saved in the running Pane app.

commands.name: repos add
commands.summary: Register an existing git repository with the running Pane app.

commands.name: panes list
commands.summary: List Pane sessions in a saved repository.

commands.name: panes create
commands.summary: Create one or more Pane sessions in a saved repository and open a terminal-backed tool tab.

commands.name: panels create
commands.summary: Create a terminal-backed tool panel inside an existing Pane session.

commands.name: panels list
commands.summary: List tool panels inside a Pane session.

commands.name: panels output
commands.summary: Read recent terminal output from a panel.

commands.name: panels screen
commands.summary: Read a compact current-screen view from a terminal panel.

commands.name: panels input
commands.summary: Send input bytes to a terminal panel.

commands.name: panels submit
commands.summary: Send text to a terminal panel and append a terminal Enter byte.

commands.name: panels submit-composer
commands.summary: Submit an agent composer using the panel-appropriate key sequence.

commands.name: panels wait
commands.summary: Wait for a terminal panel to initialize, become ready/idle, or contain text.

wrapper.name: --version
wrapper.description: Pane release to install or inspect.

wrapper.name: --download-dir
wrapper.description: Directory for downloaded Pane release artifacts.

wrapper.name: --pane-path
wrapper.description: Use or inspect an existing Pane executable.

wrapper.name: --dry-run
wrapper.description: Print the plan without downloading or installing.

localValue.name: --pane-dir
localValue.description: Connect to a Pane daemon using this Pane data directory.

localValue.name: --repo
localValue.description: Repository selector: active, id, exact path, or saved repository name.

localValue.name: --pane
localValue.description: Pane/session id to inspect.

localValue.name: --panel
localValue.description: Tool panel id to inspect or control.

localValue.name: --path
localValue.description: Existing git repository path to register with Pane.

localValue.name: --name
localValue.description: Name for the registered repository or created pane/session.

localValue.name: --worktree-name
localValue.description: Worktree name to request. Defaults to --name.

localValue.name: --base-branch
localValue.description: Base branch for the created worktree.

localValue.name: --agent
localValue.description: Built-in agent terminal template to open.

localValue.name: --tool-command
localValue.description: Custom terminal command to run instead of a built-in agent.

localValue.name: --title
localValue.description: Terminal tab title. Defaults to the selected agent title or Terminal.

localValue.name: --initial-input
localValue.description: Text to send to the terminal after the command is ready. --prompt is an alias.

localValue.name: --initial-input-file
localValue.description: Read initial input from a file or stdin.

localValue.name: --from-json
localValue.description: Read a full panes.create request JSON payload from a file or stdin.

localValue.name: --timeout-ms
localValue.description: Maximum time to wait for each pane creation job.

localValue.name: --ready-timeout-ms
localValue.description: Readiness wait timeout for panes create --wait-ready.

localValue.name: --concurrency
localValue.description: Accepted for forward compatibility. Pane currently serializes multi-pane session creation so queued jobs do not time out before starting.

localValue.name: --limit
localValue.description: Maximum recent output lines or records to read.

localValue.name: --for
localValue.description: Panel wait condition.

localValue.name: --contains
localValue.description: Text to wait for with panels wait --for text.

localValue.name: --interval-ms
localValue.description: Polling interval for panels wait.

localValue.name: --text
localValue.description: Text bytes to send to a terminal panel.

localValue.name: --input-file
localValue.description: Read panel input from a file or stdin.

localValue.name: --source
localValue.description: Identify whether a local-control mutation is user-initiated or agent/orchestrator-initiated.

localValue.name: --strategy
localValue.description: Composer submit key sequence strategy for panels submit-composer.

localBoolean.name: --wait-ready
localBoolean.description: Wait for created terminal panels to be ready before returning.

localBoolean.name: --no-focus
localBoolean.description: Create the pane or panel in the background without changing focus.

localBoolean.name: --focus
localBoolean.description: Explicitly focus the created pane or panel.

default: Run "runpane help panes create" for pane orchestration options.
install: pnpm dlx runpane@latest install daemon --prefer-tunnel ssh --label "VM"
setup: Opens the guided setup for desktop install, remote host setup, update, and diagnostics.
update: Updates Pane using the same artifact selection as "runpane install client".
doctor: Checks wrapper/runtime details, release metadata, installed Pane detection, and Pane daemon reachability.
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
repos list: Lists repositories saved in the running Pane app.
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
repos add: Registers an existing git repository with the running Pane app.
--name <name>                  Saved repository name; defaults to the directory name
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
--dry-run                      Validate and preview without adding the repo
panes list: Lists Pane sessions. Pass --repo to limit results to one saved repository.
--repo <selector>              active, id, exact path, or saved repository name
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panes create: Creates Pane sessions in a saved repository and opens a terminal-backed tool tab.
--repo <selector>              active, id, exact path, or saved repository name
--initial-input <text>         Text sent after the command is ready
--initial-input-file <path|->  Read initial input from a file or stdin
--wait-ready                   Wait for terminal readiness before returning
--source <user|agent>          Mark mutation source; agent implies background creation
--no-focus                     Create in the background without stealing focus
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
--dry-run                      Validate and preview without creating panes
panels list: Lists tool panels in a Pane session.
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panels output: Reads bounded recent terminal output from a panel.
--limit <count>                Maximum recent output lines or records to read
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panels input: Sends exact input bytes to a terminal panel. Include a newline in the input when you mean Enter.
--input-file <path|->          Read input from a file or stdin
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
agent-context: Prints Pane command context for coding agents without requiring a running Pane app.
--command <command>            Print the full definition for one runpane command
agents doctor: Diagnoses whether Codex or Claude is available in the same repository environment Pane will use.
--repo <selector>              active, id, exact path, or saved repository name; defaults to active
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panels screen: Reads a compact current-screen view from a terminal panel. Prefers alternate-screen/TUI output and falls back to recent scrollback.
--limit <count>                Maximum lines to return; defaults to 80
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panels submit: Sends text to a terminal panel and normalizes the final terminal Enter to CR. Use this for ordinary prompt answers and shell commands.
--input-file <path|->          Read text from a file or stdin before Enter
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
--yes                          Skip confirmation for this mutating command
panels wait: Polls a terminal panel until it is initialized, ready, idle, or contains text. Output is intentionally small and includes next-step guidance.
--for <condition>              initialized, ready, idle, or text; defaults to ready for CLI panels and idle otherwise
--contains <text>              Text required for --for text; implies --for text when omitted
--pane-dir <path>              Connect to a specific Pane data directory
panels create: Create a terminal-backed tool panel inside an existing Pane session.
--pane <pane-id>              Existing Pane session to add the panel to.
--agent <codex|claude>        Built-in agent command template to launch.
--initial-input, --prompt     Initial input to send after the tool starts.
--initial-input-file <path|-> Read initial input from a file or stdin.
--no-focus                    Create the panel in the background.
--source <user|agent>         Mark the mutation source; agent implies background creation.
--wait-ready                  Wait until the terminal tool is ready.
panels submit-composer: Submit an agent composer using the panel-appropriate key sequence.
--strategy <strategy>         Defaults to auto; Codex sends Ctrl+Enter and other panels send Enter.

maintainerRules: Treat `contracts/runpane/contract.json` as the source of truth for both wrapper packages and generated docs.
Every command, flag, platform default, artifact-selection rule, and attribution rule change must be reflected in the contract.
The npm and PyPI wrappers must expose the same command behavior unless the contract explicitly documents a package-manager-specific difference.
Root `README.md` and package READMEs should lead with one guided quick-start command. Explicit commands, package-manager variants, and flags belong in an Advanced section.
Release version bumps must keep root `package.json`, `packages/runpane`, and `packages/runpane-py` versions in sync. Run `pnpm run check:runpane-package-versions` before release.
`pnpm run test:runpane-contract` must pass before changing wrapper command parsing, help output, platform defaults, release asset selection, or generated contract artifacts.
Token-based npm or PyPI publishing is a temporary fallback. Prefer trusted publishing once the package names are reserved and trusted publishers are configured.
recommendedQuickStarts: python -m pip install runpane && python -m runpane setup
npmCommands: pnpm dlx runpane@latest install daemon --label "My Server"
npm i -g runpane && runpane install daemon --label "My Server"
pnpm add -g runpane && runpane install daemon --label "My Server"
yarn dlx runpane@latest install daemon --label "My Server"
pythonCommands: pipx run runpane install daemon --label "My Server"
packageManagerNotes: Use `pnpm dlx` for one-shot pnpm execution and `pnpm add -g` for persistent CLI installation.
Do not document `pnpm install runpane` as the public CLI install path.
commandUsages: runpane panes create --repo active --name issue-252 --agent codex --prompt "Kick off the discussion skill for issue 252" --yes
commandDescriptions: `runpane` with no arguments and `runpane setup` open an interactive wizard when stdin and stdout are TTYs. In non-interactive shells or CI, both forms must print help, common commands, and agent discovery hints, then exit successfully instead of waiting for input.
`runpane install` is an alias for `runpane install client`.
`runpane install client` downloads the selected Pane desktop artifact and installs, opens, or launches it for the current platform.
`runpane install daemon` downloads or installs Pane, resolves a stable Pane executable path, and spawns `<pane executable> --remote-setup <forwarded remote setup args>`.
The wrapper must stream Pane stdout/stderr without reformatting because `pane --remote-setup` prints the one-time `pane-remote://...` connection code.
`runpane update` uses the same release resolution and installer path as `install client`.
`runpane version` prints only wrapper package metadata and does not contact, launch, or focus the Pane app or daemon.

`runpane doctor` checks platform support, release metadata reachability, download URL selection, installed Pane detection, daemon reachability, and remote-daemon hints. Add `--json` for a machine-readable report that agents should run before mutating Pane state.
`runpane agent-context` prints a brief, token-efficient command schema for coding agents without connecting to the Pane daemon.
`runpane agent-context --command "panes create"` prints the detailed definition for one command. Add `--json` for machine-readable output.
`runpane repos list` connects to the running local Pane daemon and prints saved repository records.
`runpane repos add` registers an existing git repository with the running local Pane daemon. It does not create directories or initialize git repositories by default.
`runpane panes list` lists Pane sessions, optionally scoped to one saved repository.

`runpane panes create` connects to the running local Pane daemon, resolves the requested repository, creates Pane sessions, opens terminal-backed tool tabs, and optionally sends initial input to the started tool. Built-in agent panes and `--source agent` default to background/no-focus unless `--focus` is passed.
`runpane panels list` lists tool panels inside one Pane session.
`runpane panels output` reads bounded recent terminal output from one panel and strips common terminal control noise for agent use.
`runpane panels input` sends exact input bytes to one terminal panel. Prefer `--input-file` for newlines, Ctrl-C, quotes, or shell-sensitive text.
`runpane panes create --prompt` is an alias for `--initial-input`; request JSON and daemon payloads should use the canonical `initialInput` field.

When running from WSL while Pane is installed on Windows, the Linux wrapper may look for a missing `/tmp/pane-daemon.../daemon.sock` or resolve to a Windows shim such as Volta. In that case invoke the Windows wrapper through PowerShell from a Windows cwd, for example `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane repos list --json'`.
docs.wrapperFlagNote: The top-level `runpane --version` form prints the wrapper version. The install subcommand form `runpane install --version vX.Y.Z` selects a Pane release.
docs.localControlFlagNote: `runpane doctor --json`, `runpane repos list`, `runpane panes list`, `runpane panes create`, and `runpane panels ...` commands use or describe the local framed daemon socket/pipe for a running Pane app. `--pane-dir` points the wrapper at a non-default Pane data directory, such as `PANE_DIR=~/.pane_test` in development. `runpane agent-context` is local/offline and can be used before Pane is running. From WSL, if the user runs Windows Pane, call the Windows wrapper through `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane ...'` so the command can reach the Windows named-pipe daemon and avoid UNC cwd issues.
docs.daemonFlagNote: Unknown daemon flags should be forwarded rather than dropped so newer Pane versions can extend `--remote-setup` without requiring an immediate wrapper release. Unknown flags for non-daemon commands should fail clearly.
downloadAttribution: The npm package uses `source=npm` for all npm-registry consumers, including `npx`, `pnpm dlx`, `yarn dlx`, `bunx`, and global npm/pnpm installs.
The PyPI package uses `source=pip` for all Python consumers, including pip, pipx, uvx, and `python -m runpane`.
If the website route cannot satisfy the download, wrappers may fall back to the matching GitHub release asset and print a warning that website attribution may be incomplete for that run.
Wrappers also emit best-effort lifecycle telemetry to `https://runpane.com/api/runpane/telemetry` for command start/success/failure, download request/success/failure, and GitHub fallback usage.

Wrapper telemetry uses a persisted anonymous `install_id` in the form `install_<uuid>` and PostHog `distinct_id = install:<install_id>` so distinct wrapper users can be counted with `count(DISTINCT properties.install_id)`.
Wrapper telemetry must be disabled in CI and when `RUNPANE_TELEMETRY_DISABLED` is set, and must not include raw paths, labels, prompts, raw error text, or environment values.
publishingCredentials: Local implementation, build, and dry-run validation do not need npm or PyPI API tokens.
Release publishing should prefer npm Trusted Publishing and PyPI Trusted Publishing from GitHub Actions.

Fallback `NPM_TOKEN` or `PYPI_API_TOKEN` credentials may be used for first package reservation or manual publication only. They must be supplied through local environment variables or GitHub Actions secrets, never committed, and revoked or rotated after use.

pipHelpIncludes: python -m pip install runpane && python -m runpane setup

brief.title: Pane agent context
brief.summary: Pane lets a developer manage repositories and user-visible terminal panes. Agents can use runpane to create panes, inspect compact terminal state, wait for readiness, and submit interactive input.
rules: Start with `runpane doctor --json` to understand wrapper, platform, daemon reachability, and the next safe commands before mutating Pane state.
Use `runpane agent-context --json` for the full agent-facing CLI context, or `runpane agent-context --command <command> --json` for one detailed command definition.
Use `runpane repos list --json` to find the saved repository when unsure after doctor shows the daemon is reachable.

If WSL cannot reach Pane or `runpane` resolves to a broken Windows shim, the user may be running Windows Pane; try `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane doctor --json'`.
If the repository exists on disk but is not saved in Pane, use `runpane repos add --path <repo> --yes --json` before creating panes.
Use `runpane agents doctor --agent <codex|claude> --repo <selector> --json` when agent availability differs across host, Windows, WSL, or repo environments.
Use `runpane panes create --wait-ready` to create panes and validate initial terminal readiness in one call.

Use `runpane panels screen` for compact current state, `panels wait` for readiness/text checks, `panels submit` for ordinary Enter-submitted input, and `panels submit-composer --strategy auto` for agent composers.
Use `runpane panels input` only when exact bytes are required, such as Ctrl-C or handcrafted terminal input.
After creating panes or sending terminal input, validate with `panels wait` or bounded `panels screen` before reporting success.

help.name: help
help.summary: Show help for runpane or a specific command.
help.details: Use this when you need human-readable usage text for a command.

arguments.name: command
arguments.description: Optional command topic such as "panes create".

notes: Help text is generated from the runpane contract.

setup.name: setup
setup.summary: Open the guided setup wizard for install, remote host setup, update, and diagnostics.
setup.details: Use this for a human-driven Pane setup flow, not for unattended agent orchestration.
notes: In non-interactive shells, setup prints help and exits successfully.

install.name: install
install.summary: Install Pane on this machine or configure this machine as a remote daemon host.
install.details: Installs or launches Pane release artifacts at command runtime. `install daemon` forwards remote setup flags to Pane.

arguments.name: target
arguments.description: Install the desktop client or configure a remote daemon host.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Pane release to install.

arguments.name: --format
arguments.description: Release artifact format.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip interactive prompts where possible.

notes: Package install itself is inert; work begins only when runpane is executed.

update.name: update
update.summary: Update the Pane desktop app using the same artifact path as install client.
update.details: Resolves and installs the selected Pane client release.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Pane release to update to.

arguments.name: --format
arguments.description: Release artifact format.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print the update plan without downloading.

notes: Equivalent artifact selection to `runpane install client`.

version.name: version
version.summary: Print the runpane wrapper version without contacting, launching, or focusing Pane.
version.details: Use this to confirm which wrapper is available. App, daemon, and release diagnostics belong in doctor.

arguments.name: --pane-path
arguments.description: Ignored for version; retained only for parser compatibility.

doctor.name: doctor
doctor.summary: Run platform, release, installed Pane, daemon reachability, and remote setup diagnostics.
doctor.details: Use this first when an agent needs to understand whether Pane is installed, which wrapper/runtime is running, what daemon endpoint is expected, and whether the running Pane app is reachable. JSON mode should return a report even when the daemon is unreachable.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print a machine-readable environment report.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

arguments.name: --pane-path
arguments.description: Inspect a specific Pane executable.

arguments.name: --format
arguments.description: Release artifact format to inspect.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Print extra diagnostics.

notes: Agents should run `runpane doctor --json` before mutating Pane state.
The JSON report includes daemon reachability as data; an unreachable daemon is not a reason to skip the rest of the report.
Use `runpane agent-context --json` after doctor when full CLI context is needed.

agent-context.name: agent-context
agent-context.summary: Print token-efficient Pane command context for coding agents.
agent-context.details: Use this first when an agent needs to discover Pane primitives. It is local/offline and does not require a running Pane app.

arguments.name: --command
arguments.description: Print the detailed definition for one command, for example "panes create".

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

notes: Default output is brief so AGENTS.md can point here without bloating context.

repos list.name: repos list
repos list.summary: List repositories saved in the running Pane app.
repos list.details: Use this to find the right Pane-managed repository before creating panes. If the repo is missing, use `repos add` first.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable repository records.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Requires a running Pane app or daemon for the selected Pane data directory.

If this fails from WSL with a missing `/tmp/pane-daemon.../daemon.sock`, `volta: command not found`, or a Windows shim error, the user may be running Windows Pane. Retry via `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane repos list --json'`.
When using PowerShell from WSL, set a Windows cwd such as `$env:TEMP` or `$env:USERPROFILE` before running runpane to avoid UNC cwd issues.

repos add.name: repos add
repos add.summary: Register an existing git repository with the running Pane app.
repos add.details: Use this when the repository exists on disk but is not saved in Pane yet. It validates the path as a git repository.

arguments.name: --path
arguments.description: Existing git repository path to register with Pane.

arguments.name: --name
arguments.description: Saved repository name; defaults to the directory name.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for mutating commands.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Validate and preview without adding the repo.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: It does not create directories or initialize git repositories by default.
For a Windows Pane app managing WSL repositories, prefer a saved WSL repo from `repos list` when possible. Adding a brand-new WSL repo from Windows may require WSL-aware path handling.

panes list.name: panes list
panes list.summary: List Pane sessions, optionally scoped to a saved repository.
panes list.details: Use this after selecting a repository to find existing Pane sessions and their ids before inspecting panels.

arguments.name: --repo
arguments.description: Optional repository selector: active, id, exact path, or saved repository name.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Without --repo, this lists sessions across saved Pane repositories.

panes create.name: panes create
panes create.summary: Create one or more Pane sessions in a saved repository and open a terminal-backed tool tab.
panes create.details: Use this to set up user-visible panes for work. Select a saved repository, choose a built-in agent or custom terminal command, and optionally send initial input after the tool starts. For multi-step shell setup, pass a shell command through --tool-command rather than adding a new runpane primitive.

arguments.name: --repo
arguments.description: Repository selector: active, id, exact path, or saved repository name.

arguments.name: --name
arguments.description: Pane/session name.

arguments.name: --agent
arguments.description: Built-in agent terminal template to open.

arguments.name: --tool-command
arguments.description: Custom terminal command to run instead of a built-in agent.

arguments.name: --prompt
arguments.description: Alias for --initial-input; sends text after the command is ready.

arguments.name: --initial-input-file
arguments.description: Read initial input from a file or stdin.

arguments.name: --from-json
arguments.description: Read a full panes.create request JSON payload.

arguments.name: --source
arguments.description: Mutation source; agent implies background/no-focus creation.

arguments.name: --no-focus
arguments.description: Create the pane in the background without stealing focus.

arguments.name: --focus
arguments.description: Explicitly focus the created pane.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for mutating commands.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --wait-ready
arguments.description: Wait for each created terminal panel to be ready before returning.

arguments.name: --ready-timeout-ms
arguments.description: Readiness timeout per pane; defaults to 30000.

arguments.name: --concurrency
arguments.description: Accepted for compatibility. Pane currently serializes multi-pane session creation so queued jobs do not time out before starting.

examples: runpane panes create --repo active --name issue-257 --agent codex --prompt "Plan this issue" --yes
runpane panes create --repo active --name issue-123 --agent codex --prompt "Plan this issue" --wait-ready --yes --json
notes: At least one of --agent or --tool-command is required unless --from-json is used.
The built-in agent templates come from the runpane contract; custom terminal commands can pass agent-specific flags when requested by the user.
Use --initial-input-file for multi-line prompts or shell-sensitive initial input.
When the JSON result includes nextCommand, run it to validate that the terminal produced output before reporting success.
Multi-pane requests are created sequentially today. The --concurrency flag is accepted for compatibility, but agents should not rely on parallel creation.
For POSIX or WSL command chaining, use a custom terminal command like `bash -lc 'cmd1 && cmd2 && cmd3'`.
For Windows PowerShell command chaining, use a custom terminal command like `powershell -NoProfile -Command "cmd1; if ($LASTEXITCODE) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }; cmd2"`.

From WSL with Windows Pane, invoke through PowerShell and select the saved WSL repo by name or id, for example `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane panes create --repo "WSL Pane" --name issue-123 --agent codex --prompt "Plan this issue" --yes --json'`.
Use --wait-ready when an agent needs to verify that an agent terminal started instead of only creating a pane.
If readiness returns blocked, inspect blocked.suggestedCommand rather than guessing which prompt to answer.

panels list.name: panels list
panels list.summary: List tool panels inside a Pane session.
panels list.details: Use this to find the terminal panel id to inspect or control after creating or selecting a Pane session.

arguments.name: --pane
arguments.description: Pane/session id.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: The ids returned here are stable inputs for `panels output` and `panels input`.

panels output.name: panels output
panels output.summary: Read recent terminal output from a panel.
panels output.details: Use this to inspect recent terminal output from a terminal-backed panel without loading the full history by default. Live terminal scrollback is returned as bounded recent lines; persisted output fallback is returned as bounded records. Terminal control noise is stripped before returning text.

arguments.name: --panel
arguments.description: Tool panel id.

arguments.name: --limit
arguments.description: Maximum recent output lines or records to read. Defaults to 200.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Default output is bounded to the latest 200 lines or records. Use a larger --limit only when hasMore is true and more history is needed.
Use --json when an agent needs timestamps, record types, returnedCount, or hasMore.
The output is intended to be agent-readable, but terminal prompts and shell echoes may still appear; use the newest relevant lines before concluding success.
Prefer `panels screen` for a smaller current-state read before increasing output limits.

panels input.name: panels input
panels input.summary: Send input bytes to a terminal panel.
panels input.details: Use this to answer prompts or continue an agent inside an existing Pane terminal panel. Input is byte-oriented; choose --input-file for exact control over newlines and control characters.

arguments.name: --panel
arguments.description: Terminal panel id.

arguments.name: --text
arguments.description: Text bytes to send.

arguments.name: --input-file
arguments.description: Read input from a file or stdin.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for this mutating command.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Input is sent exactly as provided. Include a real newline byte when the terminal should receive Enter; across shells, `--input-file` is safer than `--text "...\n"`.
Use `--input-file -` or a temp file for multi-line input, quotes, Ctrl-C, or shell-sensitive text.
If interrupting a running process, send Ctrl-C first, validate/read output, then send the next command in a separate `panels input` call so bytes are not dropped.
After sending input, validate with `runpane panels output --panel <panel-id> --json` before reporting success.
Runpane records action metadata and errors for observability, but should not log full input text by default.
For ordinary text plus Enter, prefer `panels submit` so the terminal receives a CR Enter byte.

agents doctor.name: agents doctor
agents doctor.summary: Diagnose whether a built-in agent command is available in a Pane repository environment.
agents doctor.details: Use this before creating Codex or Claude panes when PATH may differ between macOS/Linux/Windows/WSL or between the wrapper shell and Pane. The check runs through Pane project context, not the wrapper process.

arguments.name: --agent
arguments.description: Built-in agent command to diagnose.

arguments.name: --repo
arguments.description: Repository selector; defaults to the active Pane repo.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: For WSL repos, install the agent inside the WSL distro Pane uses, not only on Windows.
This diagnoses built-in Codex/Claude templates only; custom commands should be validated by creating a pane and reading its screen.

panels screen.name: panels screen
panels screen.summary: Read a compact current-screen view from a terminal panel.
panels screen.details: Use this for token-safe current terminal state. It prefers active alternate-screen/TUI output, then live scrollback, then persisted output. Default output is bounded to 80 lines.

arguments.name: --panel
arguments.description: Terminal panel id.

arguments.name: --limit
arguments.description: Maximum lines to return; defaults to 80.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: Use this before `panels output` when an agent only needs the latest visible/current state.
If hasMore is true and context is missing, rerun with a larger --limit or use `panels output`.

panels submit.name: panels submit
panels submit.summary: Send text to a terminal panel and append a terminal Enter byte.
panels submit.details: Use this for ordinary interactive submissions. The daemon normalizes a final LF or CRLF to CR, or appends CR if no newline is present. Exact byte workflows remain on `panels input`.

arguments.name: --panel
arguments.description: Terminal panel id.

arguments.name: --text
arguments.description: Text to submit before Enter.

arguments.name: --input-file
arguments.description: Read text from a file or stdin before Enter.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for this mutating command.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: The response includes nextCommand for validation. Run it before reporting that the input worked.
Use `panels input` for Ctrl-C, escape sequences, or any workflow requiring exact bytes.

panels wait.name: panels wait
panels wait.summary: Wait for a terminal panel to initialize, become ready/idle, or contain text.
panels wait.details: Use this to validate asynchronous terminal behavior without pulling large scrollback. It returns brief readiness state, blocker hints, a compact screen, and a next command.

arguments.name: --panel
arguments.description: Terminal panel id.

arguments.name: --for
arguments.description: Condition to wait for. Defaults to ready for CLI panels and idle otherwise.

arguments.name: --contains
arguments.description: Text required for --for text; implies --for text when --for is omitted.

arguments.name: --timeout-ms
arguments.description: Wait timeout; defaults to 30000.

arguments.name: --interval-ms
arguments.description: Polling interval; defaults to 500.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

arguments.name: --pane-dir
arguments.description: Connect to a specific Pane data directory.

notes: If blocked is present, do not assume success. Use blocked.suggestedCommand or inspect `panels screen`.
The default timeout and screen are intentionally small for agent context safety.

panels create.name: panels create
panels create.summary: Create a terminal-backed tool panel inside an existing pane.
panels create.details: Use this to add reviewer or helper tabs to the current pane without creating a new session. Agent/orchestrator-created panels should pass --source agent or --no-focus so the user stays in the implementation tab. The daemon initializes the terminal immediately and can wait for CLI readiness.

arguments.name: --pane
arguments.description: Existing Pane session id to add the panel to.

arguments.name: --agent
arguments.description: Built-in agent command template to launch.

arguments.name: --tool-command
arguments.description: Custom terminal command to launch instead of a built-in agent.

arguments.name: --title
arguments.description: Panel title override.

arguments.name: --initial-input
arguments.description: Initial input to send after the tool starts.

arguments.name: --initial-input-file
arguments.description: Read initial input from a file or stdin.

arguments.name: --source
arguments.description: Mutation source; agent implies background creation.

arguments.name: --no-focus
arguments.description: Create the panel in the background without changing active panel.

arguments.name: --focus
arguments.description: Explicitly focus the created panel.

arguments.name: --wait-ready
arguments.description: Wait until the terminal tool is ready.

arguments.name: --ready-timeout-ms
arguments.description: Readiness wait timeout.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for this mutating command.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

notes: Use this for same-pane reviewer loops after PR creation/testing.
For agent-created panels, prefer --source agent or --no-focus to avoid stealing focus from the user or implementation tab.

panels submit-composer.name: panels submit-composer
panels submit-composer.summary: Submit an agent composer using the panel-appropriate key sequence.
panels submit-composer.details: Use this after sending text into an agent composer when plain Enter is not the right submit action. Agents should use --strategy auto; Pane owns per-agent submit sequences internally and verifies visible composer state when possible.

arguments.name: --panel
arguments.description: Terminal panel id.

arguments.name: --strategy
arguments.description: Composer submit key sequence strategy.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation for this mutating command.

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Print machine-readable output.

notes: Use `panels input` or `panels submit` to write prompt text first; this command only submits the current composer.
Use --strategy auto for agent workflows; explicit strategies are diagnostic escape hatches.
The JSON result includes sequenceName and verifiedSubmitted. If ok is false, follow blocked.suggestedCommand instead of assuming submission happened.

managedBlock: The developer is using Pane for this repository. Pane can manage saved repositories and create user-visible panes with terminal-backed tools for planning, discussion, implementation, and review work.
Start with `runpane doctor --json` before taking Pane actions. Use it to understand wrapper/runtime details, daemon reachability, and the next safe commands.
Use `runpane agent-context --json` for full Pane CLI context. Use `runpane agent-context --command "panels wait" --json` or another command name for detailed schema only when needed.

Default to context-safe validation: after creating panes or sending terminal input, run `runpane panels wait` or `runpane panels screen` before reporting success. Prefer `runpane panels submit` for normal text plus Enter; use `runpane panels input` only for exact bytes such as Ctrl-C or escape sequences.

WSL note: if `runpane doctor --json` cannot find `/tmp/pane-daemon.../daemon.sock` or `runpane` resolves to a broken Windows shim, Pane may be running on Windows. Try `powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Set-Location $env:TEMP; runpane doctor --json'`, then create panes through the same PowerShell form using the saved WSL repo name or id. Use `runpane agents doctor --agent codex --repo <selector> --json` to diagnose the repo environment Pane will actually use.
